title: leucaena esculenta
intro: Leucaena esculenta, also known as Marama grass or Pigeon pea, is a leguminous plant native to tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Leucaena esculenta include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of diabetes. The plant can be prepared as a tea, powder, or capsule, and is also used in traditional herbal preparations such as tinctures and infusions.
